pi_items.source: Task description (line 34 of setup_task.sh specifies this exact name). Configuration value for NextGen Connect channel.
Task description states '15 seconds (15000 ms)' at line 36 of setup_task.sh and confirmed in export_result.sh polling frequency parsing. Standard polling interval.
Task description and confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 16) and export_result.sh (line 81). Valid Linux filesystem path.

Task description and setup_task.sh line 38 specify 'MSH-3 (Sending App): HEARTBEAT_MONITOR'. Verified against HL7 v2.5 standard where MSH-3 field contains Sending Application. Correct field mapping per HL7 spec.

Task description and setup_task.sh line 37 specify 'HL7 v2.5 ADT^A01 message'. ADT^A01 is a valid HL7 v2.5 message type meaning 'Admission' event. Verified against HL7 standard where ADT-A01 is used for patient admission notifications. MSH-9 field contains message type per HL7 spec.

Task description and setup_task.sh line 39 specify 'PID-3 (Patient ID): HEARTBEAT001'. Verified against HL7 v2.5 standard where PID-3 contains Patient Identifier List (medical record number). Correct field mapping per HL7 spec.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a NextGen Connect channel named 'Heartbeat_Monitor' with JavaScript Reader source (polling every 15 seconds) that generates valid HL7 v2.5 ADT^A01 messages with Sending Application (MSH-3) set to 'HEARTBEAT_MONITOR' and Patient ID (PID-3) set to 'HEARTBEAT001'. These messages are written to /opt/heartbeat_output/ directory via File Writer destination. All metadata values are verified against HL7 v2.5 standard specifications and NextGen Connect (Mirth Connect) capabilities which officially support both JavaScript Reader connectors for scheduled message generation and File Writer destinations for filesystem output.
